defaults.tone: plainspoken, helpful, trusted small family farm brand

NWS_014.audience: dog owners with yellow lawn spots, pet areas, kennels, patios, and turf runs
NWS_014.angle: soil-first lawn recovery instead of green dye or cover-up
NWS_014.tone: friendly pet owner, practical lawn help, clear CTA
NWS_014.cta: For the lawn repair directions, visit Nature's Way Soil.
hooks: Dog spots are not just a color problem — they are a soil stress problem.
If your lawn keeps getting yellow dog spots, the soil needs support, not a quick cover-up.
For pet lawns, the best fix starts below the grass blade.

NWS_011.audience: gardeners and lawn owners dealing with tired soil, nutrient lockout, and weak roots
NWS_011.angle: humic, fulvic, and kelp support the root zone and nutrient movement
NWS_011.tone: soil educator, clear, credible, clear CTA
NWS_011.cta: Learn more at Nature's Way Soil.
hooks: If plants are fed but still look stuck, the issue may be the soil, not the fertilizer.
Humic, fulvic, and kelp help support the part of the plant you cannot see — the root zone.
Better plant growth starts with better soil movement.

NWS_013.audience: raised bed, container, and garden growers who want a concentrated soil amendment
NWS_013.angle: worm castings, biochar, compost, and duckweed extract for living soil support
NWS_013.tone: warm family farm, soil-building focus, clear CTA
NWS_013.cta: See how to use it at Nature's Way Soil.
hooks: This is not ordinary bagged dirt — it is a concentrated soil-building blend.
Healthy gardens start with living soil, not just more fertilizer.
For raised beds and containers, a little biology can go a long way.

NWS_021.audience: landowners, small farms, horse owners, cattle owners, hay and pasture managers
NWS_021.angle: liquid pasture and lawn support for stronger-looking grass and better soil support
NWS_021.cta: For hay and pasture use directions, visit Nature's Way Soil.
hooks: Pasture grass needs more than a quick green-up — it needs soil support.
If your hay field or pasture looks tired, start with the soil under the stand.
For hay, pasture, and lawn, stronger grass starts in the root zone.

NWS_018.audience: homeowners wanting greener turf and better root-zone support
NWS_018.angle: seaweed extract plus humic acid for turf and soil support
NWS_018.tone: clean lawn-care educator, premium but simple, clear CTA
NWS_018.cta: See the lawn treatment details at Nature's Way Soil.
hooks: Greener turf starts with the root zone, not just the leaf blade.
Seaweed and humic acid are a simple way to support tired lawn soil.
If your lawn looks hungry, the soil may need help moving nutrients.
